products.menuLine: Maker's Mark Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $32 · 90 proof (45.0% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: vanilla, caramel

guidance.bestFor: Wheated bourbon fan, gift pour, red-wax recognition, medium cigar.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants high-rye spice bomb only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants wheated sweetness with red-wax credibility — honey, vanilla, and soft spice.
guidance.memberBlurb: Maker's Mark — wheated bourbon with the red wax everyone recognizes. Honey and vanilla over orange peel when rye feels too sharp.

products.menuLine: Mars Iwai Tradition · Japan · $45 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, sherry

guidance.bestFor: Japanese whisky on a budget, sherry profile, medium cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when Japanese sherry character is wanted without flagship pricing.
guidance.memberBlurb: Mars Iwai Tradition — sherry and plum in an approachable Japanese blend. Smart pour when the allocated shelves are empty.

products.menuLine: Martell Blue Swift · Cognac, France · $55 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: vanilla, grape

guidance.bestFor: Member likes bourbon and cognac, crossover curious.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member loves bourbon and wants to try cognac — vanilla, caramel, familiar oak.
guidance.memberBlurb: Martell Blue Swift — cognac finished in bourbon barrels. The bridge pour for bourbon drinkers who are cognac-curious.

products.menuLine: Martell Cordon Bleu · Cognac, France · $220 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: fig, jasmine

guidance.bestFor: Cognac collector, floral-fruity luxury, maduro night.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants classic Martell luxury — fig, jasmine, and fruit cake depth.
guidance.memberBlurb: Martell Cordon Bleu — fig and jasmine in a cognac classic. Luxury for members who already know Hennessy and Rémy XO.

products.menuLine: Michter's US*1 Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $48 · 91.4 proof (45.7% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: stone fruit, vanilla

guidance.bestFor: Polished bourbon request, gift pour, medium-full cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants premium Kentucky bourbon — stone fruit, honey, and polished oak.
guidance.memberBlurb: Michter's US*1 Bourbon — stone fruit and honey over polished oak. Small-batch Kentucky that reads luxury without Van Winkle theater.

products.menuLine: Michter's US*1 Straight Rye · Kentucky · $48 · 84.8 proof (42.4% ABV) · Journey: Intermediate · Notes: citrus, rye spice

guidance.bestFor: Rye upgrade from Sazerac, polished medium-full pairing.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants premium rye — citrus, spice, and polished oak.
guidance.memberBlurb: Michter's US*1 Rye — citrus and spice in a polished small-batch rye. Premium without the WhistlePig ticket.

products.menuLine: Monkey Shoulder Blended Malt Scotch · Scotland · $35 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: vanilla, orange

guidance.bestFor: Scotch beginner, cocktail crowd, approachable malt.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants approachable malt — vanilla, orange, and honey without peat fear.
guidance.memberBlurb: Monkey Shoulder — vanilla and orange across blended Speyside malts. Scotch for people who think they do not like Scotch.

products.menuLine: Montelobos Espadín Mezcal · Oaxaca, Mexico · $35 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: smoke, agave

guidance.bestFor: Mezcal second step, balanced smoke, medium cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when Del Maguey was too intense — balanced espadín smoke and citrus.
guidance.memberBlurb: Montelobos Espadín — balanced smoke and green pepper. Mezcal for members who want smoke without going full campfire.
